fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 74 uses `>` (strictly greater than) instead of `>=`, so when `len(active) == limit` the function incorrectly returns True with 0 remaining instead of False.

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit, the branch condition should be true (deny), but with > it is false — this expr is FALSE on failing tests where len(active)==limit.
After the else branch (allowed path), active count must be strictly less than limit; equality should have been caught by the if-branch.
Sanity check that active count is comparable to limit — always true, but ensures active__1 is properly defined before the boundary check.
The returned allowed flag should be False whenever active count is >= limit (not just > limit).
